domain_name: Satellite Remote Sensing and ML for Water/Land Feature Mapping

background_concepts.concept: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imaging and backscatter
background_concepts.why_needed: SAR-based feature extraction papers assume familiarity with side-looking active radar, backscatter, and all-weather imaging without explaining the imaging physics.

suggested_resources.title: An Introduction to Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and its Applications (NASA ARSET)
Introduction to SAR (ArcGIS Pro documentation)

domain_name: Precipitation, Weather, and Climate Forecasting via Deep Learning

background_concepts.why_needed: Data-driven forecasting papers use ERA5 and similar reanalysis products as training targets/inputs, assuming the reader understands how observations and models are fused via data assimilation.

suggested_resources.title: ERA5 atmospheric reanalysis (UCAR Climate Data Guide)
Exploring the ERA5 Reanalysis (Climatematch Academy tutorial)

domain_name: Flood and Drought Risk Modeling and Impact Estimation

background_concepts.concept: Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI) and drought/hydrological extreme indices
background_concepts.why_needed: Papers on hydrological extremes reference standardized drought/flood indices as target variables without defining how precipitation deficits are normalized into severity classes.

suggested_resources.title: Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI) (UCAR Climate Data Guide)
Standardized Precipitation Index User Guide (WMO-No. 1090)

domain_name: AI Flood Mapping and Forecasting from Satellite Data

background_concepts.concept: Sentinel-1/Sentinel-2 imagery and SAR backscatter for water detection
background_concepts.why_needed: Flood mapping papers rely on Sentinel-1 SAR (dark backscatter over smooth water) and Sentinel-2 optical bands without explaining sensor characteristics or why SAR sees through clouds.

suggested_resources.title: An Introduction to Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and its Applications (NASA ARSET)

background_concepts.why_needed: Flood-extent mapping is framed as pixel-wise semantic segmentation, assuming familiarity with encoder-decoder networks and per-pixel classification.

suggested_resources.title: A Beginner's guide to Deep Learning based Semantic Segmentation using Keras

domain_name: Streamflow and Hydrological Modeling with ML

background_concepts.concept: Rainfall-runoff processes and the catchment/watershed water balance
background_concepts.why_needed: Streamflow prediction papers assume the reader understands how precipitation partitions into infiltration, evapotranspiration, and runoff draining to a catchment outlet.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to Hydrologic Modeling (National Institute of Hydrology, India)

background_concepts.concept: Nash-Sutcliffe Efficiency (NSE) and streamflow evaluation metrics
background_concepts.why_needed: Model performance is reported via NSE (and KGE), which are hydrology-specific goodness-of-fit scores not defined in the papers.

suggested_resources.title: Nash–Sutcliffe efficiency: significance and evaluation (Wisdomlib overview)

domain_name: Tropical Cyclone and Extreme Storm Prediction

background_concepts.concept: Tropical cyclone intensity and the Saffir-Simpson scale
background_concepts.why_needed: Cyclone forecasting papers estimate intensity (maximum sustained wind, categories) without explaining how storms form or how intensity is classified.

suggested_resources.title: Saffir-Simpson Hurricane Scale (U.S. National Park Service / NOAA)
Tropical Cyclone Climatology (NOAA National Hurricane Center)

background_concepts.why_needed: The cyclone-rainfall correction paper improves ERA5 fields, assuming the reader knows what reanalysis is and its known biases for extremes.

suggested_resources.title: ERA5 atmospheric reanalysis (UCAR Climate Data Guide)

background_concepts.concept: Statistical downscaling and bias correction (GCMs vs local scale)
background_concepts.why_needed: These papers assume the reader knows the difference between coarse global climate model output and local scales, and why raw model output needs bias correction before use.

suggested_resources.title: Statistical downscaling and bias correction of climate model outputs (overview)
Statistical Downscaling and Bias Correction for Climate Research (Cambridge, statistical concepts chapter)

domain_name: Compound Climate Extremes and Disaster Hazard Prediction

background_concepts.concept: ERA5/reanalysis climate fields for extreme-event labeling
background_concepts.why_needed: Compound-extreme detection papers derive labels (heatwaves, droughts) from reanalysis climate variables, assuming the reader knows these gridded datasets.

suggested_resources.title: ERA5 atmospheric reanalysis (UCAR Climate Data Guide)

domain_name: AI for Climate Policy and Ecosystem Valuation

background_concepts.concept: Meteorological vs agricultural vs hydrological drought and drought indices (SPI/SPEI)
background_concepts.why_needed: Drought forecasting/classification papers distinguish drought types and use standardized indices as targets, assuming knowledge of how each index is defined and what it measures.

suggested_resources.title: Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI) (UCAR Climate Data Guide)
Standardized Precipitation Index User Guide (WMO-No. 1090)

domain_name: Weather and Climate Model Emulation and Downscaling

background_concepts.concept: Global/regional climate models and dynamical downscaling
background_concepts.why_needed: Emulation papers presume the reader knows what physical climate/weather models compute and why emulating or downscaling them is computationally motivated.

suggested_resources.title: Climate Modelling (Statistical Downscaling and Bias Correction for Climate Research, Ch. 8)

background_concepts.concept: Calibration and uncertainty quantification for probabilistic forecasts
background_concepts.why_needed: Calibration papers assume familiarity with reliability/calibration of probabilistic weather forecasts, a concept not introduced in the reading.

suggested_resources.title: ERA5 atmospheric reanalysis and ensemble uncertainty (UCAR Climate Data Guide)

domain_name: Extreme Weather Event Detection and Forecasting

background_concepts.concept: ERA5 reanalysis fields as inputs for event detection
background_concepts.why_needed: Detection models are trained on reanalysis meteorological fields, assuming the reader understands their provenance and resolution.

suggested_resources.title: ERA5 atmospheric reanalysis (UCAR Climate Data Guide)

domain_name: Satellite Land Cover and Water Body Segmentation

background_concepts.why_needed: These papers frame water-body/aquaculture/irrigation mapping as pixel-wise segmentation or LULC classification, assuming familiarity with encoder-decoder CNNs and pixel labeling.

suggested_resources.title: A Beginner's guide to Deep Learning based Semantic Segmentation using Keras

background_concepts.concept: Optical vs SAR spectral bands / cross-sensor transfer
background_concepts.why_needed: The electro-optical-to-SAR transfer and multispectral papers assume the reader understands the difference between optical multispectral imagery and radar imagery.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to SAR (ArcGIS Pro documentation)

domain_name: Social Media and Crowdsourced Disaster Response Analytics
Flood & Disaster Risk Mapping and Response Systems
Satellite Imagery Analysis for Environmental and Socio-economic Monitoring

background_concepts.concept: Vegetation greenness / spectral vegetation indices (e.g., NDVI)
background_concepts.why_needed: Vegetation-greenness forecasting papers use satellite-derived greenness indices as targets, assuming the reader knows how multispectral reflectance encodes vegetation health.

suggested_resources.title: An Introduction to SAR and Its Applications / Fundamentals of Remote Sensing (NASA ARSET)
